thinking.thinking:
The ponder is about adding `sdlc ponder merge` - a self-contained CLI + core feature. Looking at existing milestones, none of them seem to be specifically about ponder CLI improvements. This is a focused, bounded feature:

1. Two core changes to `ponder.rs` (data model fields + merge function)
2. Two CLI touch-points in `cmd/ponder.rs` (merge subcommand + show/list updates)

Scope assessment: This is a **single feature** — it touches ~2 files, has a clear bounded scope. The question is: does it fit into an existing milestone, or does it need its own?
 
The existing milestones don't align well — `ponder-ux-polish` is already released and `v23-ponder-rich-input` is in verifying status. Given how self-contained this feature is, I should probably create a new milestone for it, something like `v30-ponder-merge` that follows the naming pattern of the other versioned milestones.

text: The design is complete and well-bounded: single concern, ~2 files, CLI+core only. This warrants one new milestone with one feature.

input.command: sdlc milestone create v30-ponder-merge --title "Ponder Merge — consolidate fragmented ponder entries" --vision "Agents and humans can merge duplicate or related ponder entries into one, keeping the roadmap clean as the idea space grows." 2>&1
sdlc milestone create v30-ponder-merge --title "Ponder Merge — consolidate fragmented ponder entries" 2>&1
sdlc feature create ponder-merge-cli --title "sdlc ponder merge — CLI command and core data model" 2>&1
